case_name: Estate of Crnjak v. Lake Hosp. Sys. Inc.

summary.brief: Estate of Crnjak v. Lake Hosp. Sys. Inc.. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: In November 2018, Rose Crnjak (“Rose”) was a 68-year-old woman living alone in Eastlake, Ohio.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On the night of Saturday, November 10, 2018, Rose called her primary care doctor, Dr. Winston Ho (“Dr. Ho”) complaining of a possible urinary tract infection (“UTI”), left flank pain, and a fever.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Rose was admitted to the ER at 7:33 p.m. on Saturday, November 10, 2018.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Rose was diagnosed with a UTI and sent home with a prescription for oral antibiotics and instructions to follow up with Dr. Ho.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Nine hours later, on Sunday afternoon, hospital staff at Lake West received the results of Rose’s blood test and learned that Rose had a blood infection.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: No one contacted Rose to inform her that she had a blood infection or otherwise instruct her.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Four days later, Rose was discovered dead in her bed at home, having died from sepsis as a result of her blood infection.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The jury returned a verdict in favor of Crnjak against Dr. Adolph and his employer, Emergency Medicine Physicians of Lake County, Inc., finding that Dr. Adolph’s “failure to notify of test results” proximately caused Rose’s death.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The jury also returned a verdict against Lake Hospital, finding that Lake Hospital was negligent.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The jury awarded Crnjak a verdict of $6 million and apportioned fault 50% to Dr. Adolph and 50% to Lake Hospital.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Based on the foregoing, we conclude that Crnjak had standing to commence this action.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: Dr. Barlotta testified that the fact that no one followed up with Rose regarding these results constituted a breach in the standard of care.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: Dr. Barlotta presented expert testimony as to the relevant standard of care for Lake Hospital, as well as testimony that Lake Hospital breached this standard of care.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

decision_points.prompt: What should be done after receiving critical lab results indicating a blood infection?

options.description: Contact patient immediately and advise return to hospital
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard of care requires notifying patient of critical results

options.description: Do not contact patient and assume follow-up will occur
options.clinical_reasoning: Failure to notify patient of critical condition

expected_action_court.description: Patient should have been contacted and advised to return
